The '''Battle of Aiqin 4''' was a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] effort to capture [[Airam (group)|Airam]] clan leader [[Ilay]] during a meeting with the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Rage (Imperial-class)|''Rage]]''.

==The Battle==
[[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue]] and [[Green Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Green Squadrons]] performed a [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump into the [[Aiqin 4 system]] just in time to see the [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class cruiser]] [[Kala'din (starship)|''Kala'din'']] arrive. However, the Star Destroyer ''Rage'' was positioned between the ''Dreadnaught'' and the Rebel strike team, forcing Rogue Squadron to engage the ''Rage''{{'s}} fighter screen prematurely, while Green Squadron made a run for the Airam cruiser in its [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing assault starfighters]].

Unloading a few [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] onto both Dreadnaught and Star Destroyer, the B-wings brought down the shields of ''Kala'din'' relatively quickly. Green Squadron proceeded to disable the ship, then turned its attention to the ''Rage''. Rogue Squadron fought valiantly to fend off any attackers bothering the B-wings during their attack runs. The ''Rage'' absorbed a beating from the [[Slayn & Korpil/Legends|Slayn & Korpil]] starfighters, but was ultimately forced to withdraw from the system or risk suffering heavy damage.

Meanwhile, the Rogues dueled both Imperial and Airam starfighters to protect the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|Rebel blockade runner]] [[Storm (CR90 corvette)|''Storm'']] during its boarding operation. [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Alliance Special Forces]] succeeded in fighting their way through the disabled ''Kala'din'' and capturing Ilay, who was taken on board the ''Storm'' as a prisoner of the Alliance. After the successful capture of the Imperial-aligned Airam leader, the ''Storm'' proceeded to exit the system. Even the late arrival of the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Vengeance (Executor-class)|''Vengeance'']] could not prevent the Rebel corvette from completing its mission. When the ''Storm'' finally entered hyperspace, Rogue and Green Squadrons withdrew, avoiding further losses in the face of rapidly mounting Imperial opposition.

==Aftermath==
Ilay's capture provided a wealth of information for clan leader [[Tamaron]] and the Rebel Alliance. One piece of information was that conscripts from Ilay's clan would be sent to the ''Vengeance'' soon, accompanied by fresh supplies. How desperate the supply situation of [[Task Force Vengeance]] had become was shown by ''Rage''{{'s}} deployment of [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] barren of [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]] against the rebel starfighter force.

==Behind the scenes==
The name of the Aiqin system is somewhat in dispute. ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter: Balance of Power Campaigns]]'', the computer game in which this battle takes place, calls it the Aiqin 4 system. This is somewhat unusual nomenclature for a [[Star system/Legends|star system]], which may contain planets or stars numbered by orbit or otherwise, but generally contains no numerals; furthermore, most numbered planetary bodies in the Star Wars universe are done so using the Romanic number system ("IV" instead of "4"). ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' names the system as simply the Aiquin system.
